1.speaker: male
1.text: Pick it up, pick it up — okay, no, your end.

1.speaker: male
1.text: I am picking it up.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Higher.

1.speaker: male
1.text: It is at my chest.

1.speaker: male
1.text: It needs to clear the railing.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Then the railing's the problem, not me.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Buddy.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Don't "buddy" me, I will drop this on you.

1.speaker: male
1.text: You will not drop this on me, you bought it on Facebook Marketplace and you love it.

1.speaker: male
1.text: I'm rethinking the love.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Step. Step. Other foot.

1.speaker: male
1.text: I have two feet, I know which one I just used.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Then why'd you use the same one twice.

1.speaker: male
1.text: …did I.

1.speaker: male
1.text: You absolutely did.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Okay, set it down. Set it down, I need a second.

1.speaker: male
1.text: We are six stairs from your apartment.

1.speaker: male
1.text: I am aware of where we are.

1.speaker: male
1.text: I'm just naming it.

1.speaker: male
1.text: Name it quieter.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Is that your dog or are you being held hostage by it?

2.speaker: male
2.text: You said 'woah' because he stepped on your shoe.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Only when attacked by park animals.

2.speaker: male
2.text: He's not attacking you, he likes you.

2.speaker: male
2.text: You're in a weird mood today.

2.speaker: male
2.text: I just watched a squirrel steal half my sandwich.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Was one of the witnesses the squirrel?

2.speaker: male
2.text: You know, your dog has better conversational skills than you.

2.speaker: male
2.text: Yeah, but he still chose to walk over here.

3.speaker: male
3.text: You don't get to invent weather to defend your team.

3.speaker: male
3.text: Then how am I supposed to defend them.

3.speaker: male
3.text: You're not supposed to. They're losing.

3.speaker: male
3.text: Can a tragedy be a comeback story.

3.speaker: male
3.text: Can your team make a basket.

4.speaker: male
4.text: I pick up like eighty percent of the time.

4.speaker: male
4.text: Pick up a hundred this week.

4.speaker: male
4.text: You don't have to do this.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Two sides of bread, meat in the middle, that's a sandwich.

5.speaker: male
5.text: It's one piece of bread, folded.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Folded does not count or a taco would be a sandwich.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Name a reasonable person who thinks a taco is a sandwich.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Tacos are tacos. Hot dogs are hot dogs. Sandwiches are sandwiches.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Spoken like a man with no convictions.

5.speaker: male
5.text: Spoken like a man who's wrong about food.

5.speaker: male
5.text: That's not how being right works.

6.speaker: male
6.text: When were you gonna tell me.

6.speaker: male
6.text: Does it matter who told me.

6.speaker: male
6.text: When were you gonna tell me.

6.speaker: male
6.text: I was figuring out how to —

7.speaker: male
7.text: I am looking at it. We're not taking it.

7.speaker: male
7.text: The GPS is sometimes wrong and you know this.

7.speaker: male
7.text: It was its fault and you defended it.

7.speaker: male
7.text: You drove us into a lake out of loyalty to your phone.

7.speaker: male
7.text: I didn't drive INTO the lake.

8.speaker: male
8.text: I just need to get on.

8.speaker: male
8.text: Talk to me about something else.

8.speaker: male
8.text: Did I tell you my neighbor stole my package.

9.speaker: male
9.text: I have nothing, I told you, I had something and I lost it.

9.speaker: male
9.text: How do you LOSE a speech.

9.speaker: male
9.text: I had it on my phone, the phone died.

9.speaker: male
9.text: You're giving the speech in five minutes.

9.speaker: male
9.text: The story you always tell. About the bike.

9.speaker: male
9.text: I'm not telling the bike story at a wedding.

10.speaker: male
10.text: We are committed because you said yes to your mom.

10.speaker: male
10.text: She wanted the piano in the living room.

10.speaker: male
10.text: It is in the living room.

10.speaker: male
10.text: It's in the wrong PART of the living room.

10.speaker: male
10.text: Your limit is higher than this.

11.speaker: male
11.text: I didn't think it would be THIS scary.

11.speaker: male
11.text: I know horror is the genre.

11.speaker: male
11.text: Why did you think it was branding.

11.speaker: male
11.text: I thought we were doing campy.

11.speaker: male
11.text: This is the opposite of campy.

11.speaker: male
11.text: I have figured that out, yes.

11.speaker: male
11.text: Do not pause on her face.

11.speaker: male
11.text: Why is the remote like this.

11.speaker: male
11.text: Don't yell, you'll wake my roommate.
